subject: Product Management
title: Smoke Tests
description: A quick win: understand Smoke Tests.

hook.text: A smoke test offers a product that doesn't fully exist yet — just enough to see if people will try to buy it.

buildup.text: The term comes from hardware testing: power on the device and see if smoke comes out. If not, keep going.

discovery.text: In product work, smoke tests include crowdfunding campaigns, pre-order pages, and waitlists — all measuring real demand.

twist.text: Pebble raised $10 million on Kickstarter for a smartwatch that was just a prototype. The smoke test funded the product.

climax.text: Smoke tests work because money talks. A credit card number is the strongest signal of intent.

punchline.text: Will they pay? That's the only question that matters.

quiz.question: What makes smoke tests the strongest form of validation?
options: They measure willingness to pay — the strongest signal of real demand
They require no effort from users
